text: 49.2g of fat. polyunsaturated fat is one of the better parts of the profile. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
16.1g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
25.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 56% daily value, vitamin b12 at 0% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
magnesium at 42% daily value, zinc at 28% daily value, iron at 25% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side: unusually protein-forward for a nut because tests whether nuts can handle an unusually high-protein member. cheap and practical because real-world advantage. strong satiety because useful practical upside.

the drawbacks next: allergy risk matters because meaningful downside. omega-3 support is weak because a big test of the current nuts philosophy. awkward category identity because botanically a legume, culturally a nut.
B tier. Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
